title: Create your first alert rule - condition

blocks.content:
Welcome to the learning path that shows you how to set your alert rule's condition. The condition is the boundary between normal and firing — it defines when the data your query returns indicates a problem worth alerting on.

In this path, you'll choose a comparison operator, set a threshold value, and preview which series would fire.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Choose the comparison operator that matches your alert
- Set a threshold value that triggers the alert
- Preview the condition against current data

## Before you begin

Before you set a condition, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- An alert rule with a query that returns data. To build one, complete the [query path](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/alerting-first-rule-query/).

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

After you set the condition, continue with the labels and evaluation, and handling and save paths to complete your rule.
